He’s cemented a place for himself in the national psyche thanks to a stellar career spanning decades in sport.

And now in news that will delight every sports nut in the country, ABC has announced that perennial favourite Bruce McAvaney OAM will be joining their 2024 Olympic Games coverage.

McAvaney was a mainstay on the Seven Network calling AFL and headlining the network’s Olympics coverage for many years.

He will step back into the spotlight to commentate the 2024 Paris Games for ABC radio.

“Having been involved in Olympic Games coverage since Los Angeles in 1984 and the voice behind iconic Australian winning moments like Cathy Freeman’s gold medal 400m sprint at the Sydney’s Olympics, McAvaney will bring his vast experience and passion to the call team,” an official release reads.

“I’ve always thought the Olympics is the ultimate event for an Australian sports broadcaster and to be given this opportunity by the ABC means a great deal to me,” the presenter said in a statement.

“These games will be as exciting as any.

“We have a 75-strong athletics team — our biggest we’ve ever sent overseas — and I’m very optimistic they’re ready to achieve great things.”

ABC listeners will be able to hear live daily Olympics coverage from 6.30pm until close of competition across ABC Radio and DAB+.

Their commentary also includes former Olympians Melinda Gainsford-Taylor (Athletics), Sarah Ryan (Swimming), Simon Orchard (Hockey), Brad McGee (Cycling), former Davis Cup player John Alexander (Tennis) and Matilda Amy Harrison (Football) with the ABC sports broadcasters, Quentin Hull, Corbin Middlemas, Clint Wheeldon, Ben Cameron, Ned Hall, Declan Byrne, Lauren Bordin and Robin Chipperfield also chipping in.

Satirical commentators Roy & HG (aka Greig Pickhaver and John Doyle) have also been announced as part of the commentating team.

title: ABC announce beloved sports commentator Bruce McAvaney will join their 2024 Olympic Games commentary team | PerthNow
description: The ABC has announced that perennial favourite Bruce McAvaney OAM will join their 2024 Olympic Games commentary team
